summary: The R33 shares the RB26DETT with the R32 but benefits from incremental improvements. Still a 25+ year old JDM car, so age-related issues dominate. The engine is bulletproof when maintained.

issues.name: Ceramic Turbo Failure
issues.description: Factory T28 turbos with ceramic exhaust wheels are susceptible to shattering, especially under increased boost. Replacement with steel-wheel units is recommended.

issues.name: HICAS Steering System
issues.description: The HICAS four-wheel steering system can develop leaks, sensor failures, or erratic behavior. Many owners delete the system for simplicity and predictability.

issues.name: ATTESA AWD Pump
issues.description: The ATTESA transfer case pump and solenoids age and can fail, leaving the car in rear-wheel drive only. Regular fluid changes extend the life.

issues.name: Rust
issues.description: Like all JDM imports, the R33 is susceptible to rust in common areas — wheel arches, rocker panels, rear quarters, and floor pans.

issues.name: Oil Pump Drive Collar
issues.description: The RB26 oil pump drive collar can spin, especially on modified cars with increased oil pressure demands. Preventive pinning is recommended.

buyingTips: The R33 is often the best value among GT-R generations — less desirable than R32 and R34, but mechanically excellent
HICAS delete is a common and worthwhile modification — the four-wheel steering system adds complexity with minimal benefit
Check for rust meticulously — Japanese road salt takes its toll on 25+ year old cars
Verify turbo health — listen for excessive shaft play or unusual noises under boost
Budget for a full maintenance refresh including all fluids, timing belt (if not recently done), and rubber components
